usb_only.title: Samsung Galaxy A5 setup for USB debugging
usb_only.summary: The Galaxy A5 is connected over USB, but it is still presenting only MTP or generic media visibility.
steps: Open Settings, then About device or About phone.
If Software information is shown, open it.
Tap Build number 7 times until Developer Options are enabled.
Go back to Settings and open Developer Options.
Reconnect the USB cable if needed and keep the phone in file transfer or data mode.
Approve the Allow USB debugging prompt on the phone.
troubleshooting: Samsung phones sometimes stay in MTP until the phone is unlocked during reconnection.
If the trust prompt never appears, toggle USB debugging off and back on.
Do not enable OEM unlocking yet unless ForgeOS has already confirmed the restore path and flashing plan.

adb_unauthorized.title: Approve Galaxy A5 USB debugging trust
adb_unauthorized.summary: The Galaxy A5 is visible to adb but has not yet trusted this computer.
steps: Unlock the Galaxy A5 and watch for the Allow USB debugging dialog.
Keep the phone awake until ForgeOS shows adb connected.
troubleshooting: If the prompt is missing, reconnect the cable while the phone is unlocked.
If needed, revoke USB debugging authorizations in Developer Options and try again.
